overview:
## Return Value

A random floating-point value within the range `[delta, 1.0]`, where `delta` is the ratio of the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/lowestValue] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/highestValue] properties.

## Discussion

When using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution] class directly, generated numbers are uniform within the range of the distribution. For example, in a distribution whose lowest value is `1` and highest is `20` (as produced by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/d20()] class method), `delta` is `1/20 = 0.05`; that is, the distribution returns numbers in 5% increments. Subclasses of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution] can alter this quantization, as well as relative probability of generating any particular number within the distribution’s range.

# nextUniform()

**Instance Method**

Generates and returns a new random floating-point value within the characteristics of the distribution.

## Declaration

```swift
func nextUniform() -> Float
```

## Return Value

A random floating-point value within the range `[delta, 1.0]`, where `delta` is the ratio of the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/lowestValue] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/highestValue] properties.

## Discussion

When using the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution] class directly, generated numbers are uniform within the range of the distribution. For example, in a distribution whose lowest value is `1` and highest is `20` (as produced by the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution/d20()] class method), `delta` is `1/20 = 0.05`; that is, the distribution returns numbers in 5% increments. Subclasses of [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKRandomDistribution] can alter this quantization, as well as relative probability of generating any particular number within the distribution’s range.

## Generating Random Numbers

- **nextInt()**: Generates and returns a new random integer within the bounds of the distribution.
- **nextInt(upperBound:)**: Generates and returns a new random integer within the bounds of the distribution and less than the specified limit.
- **nextBool()**: Generates and returns a new random Boolean value within the characteristics of the distribution.
